title: verbesina palmeri subsp. oligocephala
intro: Verbesina palmeri subsp. oligocephala, also known as a subspecies of the Verbesina palmeri plant, is a type of flowering plant in the aster family that is native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Mexico, for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Verbesina palmeri subsp. oligocephala include its potential to reduce fever, treat wounds and infections, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from the leaves and flowers, which can be dried, crushed, and mixed with other herbs to create teas, tinctures, or topical creams for therapeutic use.
